marks_distribution: Class XI: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise marks for Class XI: Computer Systems and Organisation (10), Computational Thinking and Programming - I (45), Society, Law and Ethics (15).
Class XII: Theory (70), Practical (30). Unit-wise marks for Class XII: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2 (40), Computer Networks (10), Database Management (20).
question_paper_design: No specific question paper design matrix provided in the source text.
competency_weightage: Class XI: Computational Thinking and Programming - I carries highest weightage (45 marks).
Class XII: Computational Thinking and Programming – 2 carries highest weightage (40 marks).
bloom_levels: No specific Bloom's taxonomy level distribution provided in the source text.
internal_assessment: Internal assessment components are not explicitly detailed as a separate category, but practicals and projects form part of the total 30 marks for each class.
practical_assessment: Class XI: Lab Test (12 marks), Report File + Viva (10 marks - includes minimum 20 Python programs).
Class XII: Lab Test (12 marks - 8 for Python program, 4 for SQL queries), Report file (7 marks - min 15 Python programs, 5 sets of SQL queries, 4 Python-SQL connectivity programs).
project_assessment: Class XI: Project worth 8 marks. Uses most concepts learnt.
Class XII: Project worth 8 marks. Must be tangible and useful using Python file handling/Python-SQL connectivity, done in groups of two to three students.
